name: Blueberry and Cranberry-Juice Jelly with Raspberry Coulis
recipeInstructions:
This jelly makes about 1 ¼ pints (725ml).
Defrost the fruits, if frozen, and drain well. For the jelly, put the blueberries and 275ml of the cranberry juice into a saucepan, bring to the boil and simmer gently for 2 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat the remaining juice to bubbling. Let it cool just a little, then sprinkle in the gelatine granules and stir briskly until dissolved (you can tell by checking the back of the spoon for tiny globules).
Rinse the mould or bowl with cold water and shake out; do not dry properly. Fill with hot blueberries and jelly juice. Refrigerate until set, preferably overnight or early in the morning. If you want to avoid the fruits sinking to the bottom, stir the jelly occasionally before it sets completely.
Meanwhile, make the coulis: simmer raspberries, creme de cassis, or blackcurrant juice and water for 3 minutes to boil off the alcohol and soften the fruit. Push through a sieve into a bowl to form a purée; chill.
To serve, briefly dip the mould in hot water, place a serving plate on top and invert carefully. Tap the bottom of the mould to release the jelly. Pour a rim of coulis around the base and arrange fruit artistically. Serve with extra coulis and fromage frais.
